evals.query: I have a Kora component that starts a background server, but nothing depends on it and it never runs at startup. How do I make Kora always instantiate it?
expected_behavior: Explains that Kora only instantiates components that are dependencies of others, so the worker must be marked @Root from ru.tinkoff.kora.common.annotation.Root
Annotates the @Component class (or a @KoraApp/@Module factory method) with @Root
Suggests implementing Lifecycle so init() starts the server and release() stops it
Does not use field injection or external-framework lifecycle annotations such as @PostConstruct

evals.query: Create a Kora component that opens a connection pool on startup and closes it cleanly on shutdown.
expected_behavior: Implements ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph.Lifecycle with init() and release() (both throws Exception)
Acquires resources in init() and releases them in release()
Notes that release runs on SIGTERM graceful shutdown in reverse order of init
Does not rely on external-framework lifecycle callbacks; uses the Kora Lifecycle interface only

evals.query: I have two implementations of a Cache interface in my Kora container and the build fails with an ambiguous dependency. How do I pick which one each consumer gets?
expected_behavior: Introduces marker tag classes and annotates each implementation with @Tag(SomeTag.class) from ru.tinkoff.kora.common.Tag
Requests the desired implementation at the injection point with a matching @Tag on the constructor parameter
Explains the tag is a class (not a string) for refactor-safe navigation
Does not use external-framework qualifier or primary annotations; relies on @Tag marker classes

evals.query: I want a Kora service that receives every Notifier implementation registered in the container and broadcasts to all of them.
expected_behavior: Injects All<Notifier> from ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph.All in the constructor
Notes that All<T> extends List<T> and can be assigned to a List field
Mentions that plain All<T> collects untagged components, while @Tag(Tag.Any.class) All<T> collects every implementation including tagged ones
Does not use classpath component scanning or reflective context lookups

evals.query: My Kora CacheManager only sees the untagged caches. I need it to invalidate ALL caches, including the ones registered under tags.
expected_behavior: Uses @Tag(Tag.Any.class) on the injected collection to gather both tagged and untagged components
Injects @Tag(Tag.Any.class) All<Cache> or @Tag(Tag.Any.class) List<Cache>
Explains that a tag-less All<T> deliberately excludes tagged components, which is why some caches were missing
Does not use qualifier wildcards or reflective container lookup

evals.query: In my Kora service an AnalyticsTracker dependency may or may not be present in the container. The graph build fails when it is absent. How do I make it optional?
expected_behavior: Marks the constructor parameter with a @Nullable annotation (jakarta/javax/org.jetbrains) so the graph does not fail when the component is missing
Adds a null check before using the optional dependency
Notes that without @Nullable the compile-time graph requires the component
Does not use required=false autowiring or Optional<T> injection; uses a @Nullable parameter

evals.query: My Kora ApiClient gets recreated every time its AuthConfig refreshes, which is expensive. I want the client to read the latest config without being rebuilt.
expected_behavior: Injects ValueOf<AuthConfig> from ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph.ValueOf instead of AuthConfig directly
Calls config.get() inside the request path to read the current instance
Explains that ValueOf decouples lifecycles so the consumer is not refreshed when the wrapped component changes, and can also break circular dependencies
Does not use @Lazy, ObjectProvider, or a refresh-scope annotation from another framework

evals.query: I want to wrap my Kora JdbcDatabase component with cache-warming logic during container startup, before anything else uses it.
expected_behavior: Implements GraphInterceptor<JdbcDatabase> from ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph.GraphInterceptor as a @Component
Performs the work in init(value) and returns the (possibly wrapped) instance that other components will receive
Notes the contract mirrors Lifecycle except init/release return the component instance
Does not use a bean post-processor or runtime AOP proxy from another framework

evals.query: I provide a third-party DataSource from a @Module factory method, but it needs init/release hooks and the class cannot implement Lifecycle. How do I give it a lifecycle in Kora?
expected_behavior: Returns Wrapped<DataSource> from the factory method using new LifecycleWrapper<>(instance, initFn, releaseFn)
Imports ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph.LifecycleWrapper and ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph.Wrapped
Explains LifecycleWrapper is the factory-method equivalent of implementing Lifecycle on the class
Does not rely on external-framework init/destroy method factory attributes
